question: Does the model's final textual answer explicitly state the path sequence as 'up, right, up' (or an equivalent semantic description like 'move North, then East, then North')?
Did the model's final generated image successfully visualize a continuous path connecting the green 'S' (Start) square to the pink 'E' (End) square?
Is the model's final path the optimal shortest path, consisting of exactly 3 moves?
Does the final answer strictly adhere to movement constraints, avoiding diagonals and not passing through any black wall segments?
In the generated image, is the drawn path clearly distinguishable from the maze background (e.g., using a contrasting color or line thickness)?
In the generated image, is the drawn path strictly contained within the open corridors/white space, avoiding any overlap with black wall lines?
In the generated image, does the drawn path strictly avoid crossing the vertical black wall located immediately to the right of the Start (S) cell?
In the generated image, does the path enter the red 'E' zone strictly from the bottom, avoiding the solid vertical walls on the left and right boundaries of the 'E' cell?
Is the underlying maze structure (wall layout, S and E locations) preserved in the generated images without fabricated openings or missing walls?
Does the drawn path originate strictly inside the 'S' cell and terminate strictly inside the 'E' cell (not stopping short or overshooting)?
Does the path visualization correctly depict exactly three orthogonal segments corresponding to the sequence: vertical (up), horizontal (right), vertical (up)?
Does the model's reasoning demonstrate valid spatial understanding by strictly adhering to the 4-direction movement constraint (no diagonals)?
Does the generated path avoid unnecessary detours (such as entering the top-left or top-right dead-end corners) and represent the shortest route?
When the text describes the first move as 'up', does the corresponding image show the path moving vertically upward from the S cell?
When the text describes the second move as 'right', does the corresponding image show the path moving horizontally to the right?
When the text describes the final 'up' move, does the corresponding image show the path moving vertically upward into the E cell?
Are there any contradictions between the text and the image, such as the text saying 'turn right' while the image shows the path going straight or turning left?
